title: Light Spanish Cornmeal Pilaf
description: A malty, dense, and crowd-pleasing Spanish dish featuring white cornmeal, this pilaf is a low-fat friendly option that is perfect for serving in a tagliere vessel. It is a great option for those looking for a flavorful and healthy side dish.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 375 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a large tagliere or baking dish, heat the olive oil over medium heat.
Add the diced onion and cook until it is translucent and starting to caramelize.
Add the minced garlic and cook for an additional minute.
Stir in the smoked paprika, salt, and black pepper.
Add the white cornmeal and cook for 1-2 minutes, stirring constantly.
Gradually add the chicken broth, stirring constantly to prevent lumps.
Bring the mixture to a boil, then cover the dish with aluminum foil and transfer it to the preheated oven.
Bake for 25-30 minutes, or until the liquid has been absorbed and the cornmeal is tender.
Remove the dish from the oven and stir in the chopped fresh parsley.
